ontology.foodon_gap_note: No dedicated FoodOn URI exists for taiyaki specifically; 'waffle food product' is the closest defensible FoodOn class for a batter-based griddle-cooked item. Taiyaki is a fish-shaped batter cake, not a true waffle, so this is an approximate match.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJX (Pancakes and similar products) is the closest defensible category for taiyaki, a batter-based filled cake. No dedicated FoodEx2 code exists for taiyaki.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No defensible SNOMED CT code identified for taiyaki or equivalent batter-based filled confection.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard taiyaki piece weighs approximately 100 g and constitutes a typical single-serve snack portion.

nutrient_source.title: Taiyaki (red bean) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record carries HPB provenance at confidence 0.95 for all core macros and micronutrients. Values are accepted as sourced from HPB Foodo. Saturated fat and sugar are not reported by HPB for this entry; these are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for 'Pancakes, plain, prepared from recipe' (FDC ID 172163) and 'Candies, sweet chocolate' analogy for sweetened red bean paste sugar content, documented below in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for taiyaki exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 65 is estimated by analogy to wheat-flour pancake / waffle products with sweetened filling. Plain pancakes have a reported GI of approximately 67 (University of Sydney GI Database). The sweetened red bean paste filling has a moderate-to-high sugar content (~18 g/100 g) but also contains some residual starch from red beans. A composite GI of 65 (medium) is defensible for this product. GL per serving = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 46.0) / 100 = 29.9 for a 100 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (estimated 18 g/100 g) with very low dietary fibre (0.4 g/100 g) triggers insulin-mediated sodium retention; sodium at 280 mg/100 g is also borderline elevated for a snack food.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit taiyaki due to its high sugar load (18 g/100 g) and moderate sodium (280 mg/100 g), as the combination promotes insulin-mediated sodium retention and elevates blood pressure risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High estimated sugar (18 g/100 g) combined with very low dietary fibre (0.4 g/100 g) and a medium-high glycaemic index (~65) produces a high glycaemic load of ~29.9 per 100 g serving, causing rapid postprandial glucose elevation.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid taiyaki; the high sugar content from sweetened red bean paste, negligible fibre, and high glycaemic load (GL ~30 per serving) will cause significant postprandial blood glucose spikes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate fat content (6.9 g/100 g) with estimated saturated fat ~1.8 g/100 g and high sugar (18 g/100 g) contribute to atherogenic risk; sodium at 280 mg/100 g adds cardiovascular load.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit taiyaki consumption due to its combined sugar, saturated fat, and sodium content, which collectively contribute to dyslipidaemia, endothelial stress, and elevated blood pressure risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated sugar ~18 g/100 g exceeds the 15 g/100 g refined sugar threshold associated with promotion of pro-inflammatory cytokines relevant to RA; refined wheat flour batter adds to the high glycaemic inflammatory load.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid taiyaki as its high refined sugar content (estimated 18 g/100 g) and low fibre promote inflammatory mediator production; substituting with unsweetened whole red bean preparations is preferred.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Taiyaki (たい焼き, anko) is a popular Japanese street snack and festival food consisting of a fish-shaped batter cake filled with sweetened red bean paste (anko). The batter is made from wheat flour, egg, sugar, and a leavening agent, poured into a fish-shaped iron mould and griddle-cooked until golden and crisp on the outside with a soft, slightly chewy interior. The filling is sweetened azuki red bean paste, which is dense, smooth, and moderately sweet. Taiyaki originated in Japan in the early 20th century and remains widely sold at street stalls, convenience stores, and Japanese-themed food outlets across Asia including Singapore. Nutritionally, a 100 g serving provides 269 kcal, 5.7 g protein, 6.9 g fat, and 46 g carbohydrate, with an estimated 18 g sugar and only 0.4 g dietary fibre. Sodium is 280 mg/100 g. The high sugar and low fibre profile produces a high glycaemic load (~30 per serving), making taiyaki unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The sweetened red bean paste, while derived from a legume, is processed and sugar-dominant in this preparation, negating the fibre and micronutrient benefits of whole red beans.

confidence_reason: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, iron) are sourced directly from HPB Foodo at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and sugar are estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous entries and documented in notes. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using wheat-flour pancake data, which is the closest approved source available.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (1.8 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry 'Pancakes, plain, prepared from recipe' (FDC ID 172163), which reports ~1.7–1.9 g saturated fat per 100 g for a comparable batter product. This is an adapted estimate; no HPB-reported saturated fat value exists for this entry. 2. Sugar (18.0 g/100 g) is estimated based on the known composition of sweetened azuki red bean paste (anko), which typically contains 40–50% sugar by weight, combined with the batter sugar contribution. The filling constitutes approximately 35–40% of the total product weight, yielding an estimated total sugar of ~18 g/100 g. This is consistent with the input record's health suitability reasoning. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for taiyaki are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central for this specific product as of 2026. 4. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on wheat-flour pancake/waffle products from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct taiyaki GI measurement is available in any approved source. 5. Halal status is assumed as typical commercial taiyaki uses plant-based filling and standard baking ingredients; confirmation from a certified halal authority is recommended for specific products.
